questions.prompt: Consider the rational function f(x) = (x² - 4)/(x² - 2x - 8). Which of the following correctly identifies all vertical asymptotes and holes?
options: A) Vertical asymptote at x = -2; hole at x = 4
B) Vertical asymptote at x = 4; hole at x = -2
C) Vertical asymptotes at x = -2 and x = 4
D) Hole at x = 2; vertical asymptote at x = -4
questions.correctAnswer: B) Vertical asymptote at x = 4; hole at x = -2
questions.explanation: To find vertical asymptotes and holes, factor both numerator and denominator. Numerator: x² - 4 = (x - 2)(x + 2). Denominator: x² - 2x - 8 = (x - 4)(x + 2). The common factor (x + 2) creates a hole at x = -2 (found by setting x + 2 = 0). The factor (x - 4) in the denominator only has no matching factor in the numerator, creating a vertical asymptote at x = 4. Holes occur where both numerator and denominator equal zero; vertical asymptotes occur where only the denominator equals zero.
